Description of the vaccine: BCG vaccine (TICE strain) is for the prevention of tuberculosis.
Dosage: Adults: 1 mL of sterile water and infants: 2 mL of sterile water for injection.
Mechanism of action: Th-1 cell mediated and activate antigen-specific CD4+ T-cells.
Indications: Prevention in persons not previously infected with M. tuberculosis and at high risk.
Export: Distributed by - Organon USA Inc. Roseland
Side effects of vaccine: Cervical lymphadenopathy, induration, pustule formation, ulceration, regional suppurative lymphadenitis with draining sinuses.
Post vaccination: Person should be tuberculin skin tested 2-3 months post vaccination.

Description of the vaccine: BCG (Bacillus Calmette-Guerin) Danish strain 1331.

Indications: Give as soon as possible after birth to prevent increased risk of TB.
Export: Marketing authorisation holder - AJ Vaccines A/S
Post vaccination: No immunisation should be given in the same arm for at least three months.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus toxoids, acellular pertussis, inactivated poliovirus, haemophilus b conjugate and hepatitis B vaccine (DTaP vaccine).
Dosage: 3-doses at 2, 4 and 6 months of age.
Mechanism of action: Serum diphtheria antitoxin level of 1 IU/ml provides long-term protection.
Indications: Prevents diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, poliomyelitis, hepatitis B, Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Export: Distributed by - Merck Sharp & Dohme Corp and Sanofi Pasteur Inc, USA
Repurposing: Also for tetanus, pertussis, polio, Hib and hepatitis B.
Side effects of vaccine: Irritability,crying ,injection site pain,erythema and swelling, somnolence,decreased appetite, fever and vomiting.

Description of the vaccine: Tetanus (reduced), Diphtheria (reduced) and Pertussis vaccine (Tdap vaccine)
Name of the manufacturer: GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals and GSK Vaccines GmbH
Dosage: 0.5 ml injection with initial dose 5 years after the Dtap or Td vaccine. And additional dose 9 years after the Tdap vaccine.
Mechanism of action: Serum diphtheria antitoxin level of greater than or equal to 1 IU/ml provides long-term protection.
Indications: Not indicated in children aged younger than 10 years.
Export: Distributed by - GlaxoSmithKline Research Triangle Park
Side effects of vaccine: Pain,redness and swelling at the injection site, increase in arm circumference,headache,fatigue and gastrointestinal symptoms.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus toxoids, acellular pertussis vaccine adsorbed (DTaP vaccine).
Name of the manufacturer: GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals and GSK Vaccines GmbH
Efficacy: Levels of diphtheria antitoxin greater than or equal to 0.01 IU/mL were achieved in 100% of the sera tested.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis (acellular component), poliomyelitis (inactivated) and Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) conjugate vaccine (adsorbed).

Description of the vaccine: DTPa/Hib vaccine.

Description of the vaccine: Tetanus and Diphtheria Toxoids Adsorbed (Td vaccine).

Repurposing: For acellular pertussis, diphtheria and inactivated poliovirus.
Side effects of vaccine: Somnolence, injection site reactions, headache, malaise and fatigue.

Description of the vaccine: Combination of tetanus toxoid and diphtheria toxoid.
Name of the manufacturer: Novartis vaccine Diagnostics GmbH & Co. KG.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Dosage: Primary Immunisation: Three doses.\nBooster: At 10 year intervals.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder : Biogenetech Co., Ltd., Thailand
Side effects of vaccine: Erythema , swelling, pain, induration, itching, myalgia, headache

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis (acellular, component) and poliomyelitis (inactivated) vaccine.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis and poliomyelitis.
Export: Marketing authorisation holder : Sanofi Pasteur Europe
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, redness, swelling, headache, diarrhoea, nausea, myalgia, fever, chills,

Description of the vaccine: Tetanus toxoid, reduced diphtheria toxoid and acellular pertussis vaccine adsorbed combined with inactivated poliomyelitis vaccine.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Efficacy: Seroprotective tetanus antitoxin levels were achieved in 100% of adults and adolescents and 100% of children.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.
Mechanism of action: Serum tetanus antitoxin level:\nat least 0.01 IU/mL: Minimum protection.
Indications: Immunization against tetanus, diphtheria, pertussis and poliomyelitis.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, swelling, pain, tired, headache, generalized body ache and sore or swollen joints.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria and tetanus toxoids and acellular pertussis vaccine adsorbed.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Efficacy: Reduces the risk of developing diphtheria and protection lasts around 10 years.
Dosage: Three doses, gap of 4-8 weeks.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis simultaneously.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site reactions, fever, irritability, drowsiness and vomiting.

Description of the vaccine: Tetanus and diphtheria toxoids adsorbed and inactivated poliomyelitis vaccine.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Dosage: Primary immunisation - Three doses.\nBooster - 0.5 mL dose at 10 year interval.
Mechanism of action: Serum tetanus antitoxin level:\nat least 0.01 IU/mL : Minimum protective level
Indications: Immunization against tetanus, diphtheria and poliomyelitis.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, tenderness, induration, swelling, pain, itchiness, muscle ache, headache, dizziness and malaise.

Description of the vaccine: Tetanus and diphtheria toxoids adsorbed.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Dosage: Primary immunisation - Three doses.\nBooster- Single dose at 10 year interval.
Mechanism of action:
Serum tetanus antitoxin level:
at least 0.01 IU/mL: Minimum protective level
at least 0.1 IU/mL : Protective
1.0 IU/mL: Long term protection
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, swelling, redness, fever, chills and sore joints.

Description of the vaccine: Prevents the symptoms of tetanus.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Dosage: Primary immunisation - Three doses.\nBooster- Single dose at 10 year interval.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, pain, fainting, mild headache, mild vomiting, mild fussiness, irritability, crying, confusion, joint pain and body ache.

Description of the vaccine: Vaccine for diphtheria and tetanus.
Bacterial strain: An obligate anaerobic bacillus, which is gram positive.
Efficacy: Protective diphtheria and tetanus antitoxin levels developed in 100% of the recipients.
Dosage: Five doses at  at 2, 4, 6, 15-18 months and between 4 and 6 years.
Mechanism of action: Serum tetanus antitoxin level of 0.01 IU/mL is minimum protective level.
Export: Distributed by: Sanofi Pasteur Inc., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Crying, redness, swelling, tenderness, fever, moderate or severe arm mobility and loss of appetite.

Description of the vaccine: Vibrio cholerae serogroup O1 vaccine.
Efficacy: 90.3% effective 10 days after vaccination and 79.5% effective 90 days after vaccination.
Dosage: Single liquid dose, 10 days before the potential exposure.
Mechanism of action: Exposure to V. cholerae causes an elevation in baseline vibriocidal antibody titers which results in immunity.
Indications: Not been shown to protect against disease caused by  V. cholerae serogroup O139 or other non-O1 serogroups.
Export: Distributed by - Emergent Travel Health Inc., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Tiredness, headache, abdominal pain, nausea, lack of appetite and diarrhoea.
Post vaccination: To avoid eating or drinking for 60 minutes before and after oral ingestion.

Description of the vaccine: Oral cholera vaccine (OCVs) used mainly by travellers. In February 2015, Dukoral was acquired by Valneva, Lyon, France.
Efficacy: Short-term protective efficacy after 2 doses was 85% and 60.5% for the booster dose.
Dosage: Primary vaccination: Two doses and children aged 2 -5 require a third dose, Booster vaccination: Single dose.
Mechanism of action: Bacterial strains of both Inaba and Ogawa serotypes and of El Tor and classical biotypes are included in the vaccine. It acts by inducing antibodies against both\nthe bacterial components and CTB.
Indications: Immunisation does not protect against V. cholerae serogroup O139 or other species of Vibrio.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder: SBL Vaccines, Sweden
Repurposing: Short-term protection against enterotoxigenic E. coli (ETEC).
Side effects of vaccine: Abdominal pain, diarrhoea, loose stools, nausea and vomiting.
Post vaccination: Food, drink and oral administration of other vaccines should be avoided 1 hour before and after vaccination.

Description of the vaccine: Vibrio cholerae vaccine consisting of killed whole-cell O1 and O139 serogroups without CTB.
Efficacy: 67% protection against V. cholerae O1 cholera.
Dosage: 1.5 mL. The primary immunization schedule includes two doses, at an interval of two weeks.
Mechanism of action: Acts locally in the gastrointestinal tract to induce an IgA antibody response.
Indications: Data for the safety and efficacy of the vaccine in infants is not available.
Side effects of vaccine: Acute Gastroenteritis, diarrhoea, fever, vomiting, abdominal pain, itching, rash, nausea, weakness, cough, vertigo, dryness of mouth.
Post vaccination: Medical supervision for at least 30 minutes after vaccination.

Description of the vaccine: Cholera vaccine containing O1 and O139 of Vibrio cholerae.
Dosage: Two doses of vaccine at an interval of two weeks.
Approval: Korean Ministry of Food and Drug Safety export license (2014) and WHO pre-qualified (2015)
Side effects of vaccine: Headache, fever, diarrhoea, nausea and myalgia.
Post vaccination: Medical supervision for at least 30 minutes after vaccination.

Description of the vaccine: World's first plastic tube presentation oral Cholera vaccine (OCV) and an improved version of Euvichol.
Dosage: Two doses of vaccine at an interval of two weeks.

Description of the vaccine: Bivalent, killed whole-cell (O1 and O139 serogroups). Licensed only in Vietnam.
Efficacy: Seroconversion rates of vibriocidal antibodies of 91% among adults in Vietnam, 53% among adults in Kolkata and 80% among children aged greater than 1 year.
Dosage: Two doses, gap of 14 days.

Description of the vaccine: Oral killed OCV containing V. cholerae O1 cells without the cholera toxin B (CTB) subunit. Licensed only in Vietnam.
Efficacy: Moderate (58%) short-term (4–6-month) protection, 60% for 2 years and was present (42%) during the third year of follow-up.
Dosage: Two doses, gap of 14 days.

Description of the vaccine: BCG has significant protective efficacy against severe TB in children and against leprosy in adults.
Efficacy: Efficacy against leprosy ranged from 26 to 41% in experimental studies to 61% in observational studies.
Dosage: Adults: 1 mL of Sterile Water for Injection added to one vial.\nInfants (less than 1 month): 2 mL of Sterile Water for Injection.
Mechanism of action: Th-1 cell mediated and activate antigen-specific CD4+ T-cells.
Export: Distributed by - Organon USA Inc. Roseland
Side effects of vaccine: Cervical lymphadenopathy, induration, pustule formation, ulceration, regional suppurative lymphadenitis with draining sinuses, caseous lesions and disseminated BCG infection.

Description of the vaccine: Live whole cell vaccine used in the countries of the Former Soviet Union (FSU) and some Asian countries.
Dosage: Single dose as annual routine vaccination.
Indications: Administered yearly in springtime among people living in plague-enzootic areas.
Export: Not commercially available in United States and Europe.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (enzootic from wild rodents and their fleas)

Description of the vaccine: Plague vaccine.
Dosage: Three primary doses and booster doses (0.2 mL) each can be administered three times at 6-month intervals when continuous high risk for exposure.
Indications: Recommended for laboratory personnel who are routinely exposed and for persons in contact with the wild-rodent hosts or their fleas.
Side effects of vaccine: Local erythema, induration at the site of injection, malaise, headache, fever and lymphadenopathy.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (enzootic from wild rodents and their fleas)

Description of the vaccine: Killed whole cell vaccine for preventing bubonic plague.
Dosage: NA
Side effects of vaccine: Malaise, headaches elevated temperature and lymphadenopathy.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (enzootic from wild rodents and their fleas)

Description of the vaccine: Pentavalent botulinum toxoid against serotypes A through E and was distributed for human use until November 1981.
Dosage: 0.5-ml dose in 0, 2, 12, 26, and 52 weeks, with annual boosts.
Mechanism of action: Prevent the binding of the toxin to an appropriate receptor and promote clearance and degradation by phagocytes.
Indications: Used for at-risk workers and for use with military personnel at-risk during deployment.
Approval: Investigational New Drug (IND 161) held by the CDC and the U.S. Army Office of Surgeon General (IND 3723)

Description of the vaccine: Botulism antitoxin bivalent (Equine) types A and B.
Dosage: For prevention - 1,500-7,500 IU of type A and 1,100-5,500 IU of type B .\nFor treatment - 7,500 IU of type A and 5,500 IU of type B.
Indications: For prevention or treatment of Botulism types A and B.
Side effects of vaccine: Anaphylactic reactions, chilly sensation, slight dyspnoea, fever, urticaria, arthritis and lymphadenopathy.

Description of the vaccine: Botulism antitoxin heptavalent (A, B, C, D, E, F, G) - (Equine).
Name of the manufacturer: Cangene Corporation, a subsidiary of Emergent BioSolutions Inc.
Efficacy: In guinea pig, 100% for neurotoxin serotype A, B, E and F, 97% in C and D.\nIn non-human primate, 47% survival rate.
Dosage: Adults: One vial\nPediatric (1 to 17 years): 20-100% of adult dose\nInfants: 10% of adult dose regardless of body weight
Mechanism of action: Passive immunization with equine polyclonal antibody fragments.
Indications: Treatment of symptomatic botulism exposure to botulinum neurotoxin serotypes A, B, C, D, E, F, or G.
Side effects of vaccine: Headache, nausea, pruritus, urticaria, pyrexia, rash, chills and edema.

Description of the vaccine: Contains the attenuated strain Salmonella typhi Ty21a.
Efficacy: In Alexandria, Egypt - 95% decrease in the incidence of typhoid fever.  In Santiago, Chile - vaccine efficacy was 29%  for the single dose schedule and 59% for the 2-dose schedule.
Dosage: Four doses of vaccine be taken at the alternate day interval. Re-immunization dose of 4 vaccine capsules taken on alternate days be given every 5 years.
Mechanism of action: Humoral anti-S. typhi LPS antibody response.
Indications: Selective immunization recommended for travellers or people in close contact with the bacteria.
Export: Distributed by - Crucell Vaccines Inc., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Abdominal pain, nausea, headache, fever, diarrhoea, vomiting and skin rash.

Description of the vaccine: Contains the attenuated strain Salmonella typhi Ty21a.
Efficacy: In Alexandria, Egypt - 95% decrease in the incidence of typhoid fever.  In Santiago, Chile - vaccine efficacy was 29%  for the single dose schedule and 59% for the 2-dose schedule.
Dosage: All 4 doses of vaccine be taken at the alternate day interval. Re-immunization dose of 4 vaccine capsules taken on alternate days be given every 5 years.
Mechanism of action: Humoral anti-S. typhi LPS antibody response.
Indications: Selective immunization recommended for travellers or people in close contact with the bacteria.
Export: Distributed by - Emergent Travel Health Inc., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Abdominal pain, nausea, headache, fever, diarrhoea, vomiting and skin rash.

Description of the vaccine: Typhoid Vi polysaccharide vaccine.
Efficacy: The protective efficacy was 74% for blood culture confirmed cases of typhoid fever during 20 months of post-vaccination follow-up.
Dosage: Single injection of 0.5 mL. A reimmunizing dose is 0.5 mL.
Mechanism of action: Production of IgG antibodies and is T cell independent.
Indications: Recommended to travellers for areas like Africa, Asia, and Central and South America.
Export: Distributed by - Sanofi Pasteur Inc., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site pain, erythema and induration.

Description of the vaccine: Vi polysaccharide typhoid vaccine.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.\nSubjects who remain at risk should be revaccinated using a single dose every 3 years.
Mechanism of action: Production of IgG antibodies and is T cell independent.
Indications: Against typhoid fever for adults and children.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, pain, swelling, fever, headache, general aches, malaise, nausea and itching.

Description of the vaccine: World’s first Typhoid conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: Protective efficacy of 87% against typhoid fever.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL and a booster dose may be given after 3 years.
Mechanism of action: T-cell dependent, induces Vi antibodies that neutralize Vi antigen.
Indications: Long term protection against typhoid fever.
Export: Marketed by : Bharat Biotech International Ltd, India
Side effects of vaccine: Pain at injection site, swelling, fever and headache.

Description of the vaccine: Formulated with Vi polysaccharide conjugated to a carrier protein (CRM197).
Dosage: Single dose and a booster dose may be given after 3 years.
Mechanism of action: Through conjugation, TCVs are able to stimulate parts of the immune system and give long lasting immunity.
Indications: Long term protection against typhoid fever.
Export: Allowed to be purchased by United Nations agencies.

Description of the vaccine: Purified Vi capsular polysaccharide of Salmonella typhi (Strain Ty2) conjugated with Tetanus toxoid protein.
Efficacy: Efficacy has been found to be 100% over a follow up period of 1 year.
Dosage: Single dose followed by booster after 2.5-3 years of primary vaccination.
Indications: Active immunization against Salmonella typhi in infants, children and adults.
Export: Marketed by: BIO-MED Private Limited, India
Approval: Licensed in India and not WHO prequalified
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, induration, erythema, purities at the injection site.

Description of the vaccine: Purified Vi polysaccharide tetanus toxoid conjugate typhoid vaccine (Vi-TT).
Efficacy: The seroconversion rate among ZyVac-TCV recipients was 94.8%.
Dosage: Two doses, second dose is given 3 years of first dose.
Mechanism of action: The tetanus toxoid acts as conjugation protein and it helps the immune system to recognize the antigens more effectively.
Indications: Active immunization to prevent typhoid fever.
Side effects of vaccine: Mild to moderate fever, injection site pain and swelling, irritability, headache, lose motions, vomiting.

Description of the vaccine: Combined purified Vi polysaccharide typhoid and inactivated Hepatitis A vaccine.
Dosage: Dose - 1 mL. Single dose for initial protection. Repeat vaccinations are required to maintain protection every 3 years.
Indications: Active immunization to prevent typhoid fever and hepatitis A.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, pain, swelling, headache, feeling unwell, soreness, muscle ache, nausea, fever and fainting.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal (Groups A, C, Y, and W-135) oligosaccharide diphtheria CRM197 conjugate vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Lyophilized MenA conjugate vaccine component reconstituted with MenCYW-135 liquid conjugate vaccine component.
Dosage:
Primary Vaccination -
2 months children : 4-dose series at 2, 4, 6, and 12 months of age.
Children initiating vaccination at 7 months through 23 months :  2-dose series.
Individuals (2 year-55 years) : a single dose of 0.5 ml.
Booster - single booster dose individuals aged 15 to 55 years after 4 years.
Mechanism of action: Production of bactericidal antibodies against the capsular polysaccharides of serogroups A, C, Y, and W-135.
Indications: To prevent invasive meningococcal caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A, C, Y and W-135. It does not prevent N meningitidis serogroup B disease.
Side effects of vaccine: For children- tenderness, injection site pain, erythema, irritability, sleepiness, persistent crying , change in eating habits, vomiting, diarrhoea, malaise and headache.\nAdolescents and adults- injection site pain, headache, myalgia, malaise, nausea.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal (Groups A, C, Y and W-135) polysaccharide diphtheria toxoid conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: Was inferred from the immunologic equivalence to Menomune – A/C/Y/W-135 vaccine as assessed by SBA.
Vaccine formulation: Solution supplied in 0.5 mL single-dose vials.
Dosage:
Primary Vaccination:
Children 9 to 23 months : Two doses, three months apart.
Individuals 2 to 55 years: A single dose.
Booster Vaccination:
Individuals 15 to 55 years : single booster dose for those at continued risk for meningococcal disease, after 4 years have elapsed.
Mechanism of action: Production of bactericidal antibodies specific to the capsular polysaccharides of serogroups A, C, Y and W-135.
Indications: To prevent invasive meningococcal disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A, C, Y and W-135. It does not prevent N meningitidis serogroup B disease.
Side effects of vaccine: Infants and toddlers 9 and 12 months :  injection site tenderness, erythema, swelling, irritability, abnormal crying, drowsiness, appetite loss, vomiting, and fever.\nIndividuals 2 -55 years : injection site pain, redness, induration, swelling, anorexia, diarrhoea, irritability, headache, fatigue, malaise and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal group B Vaccine (MenB Vaccine).
Efficacy: Was assessed by measuring serum bactericidal activity using human complement (hSBA).
Dosage: 2 doses (0.5-mL each) at least 1 month apart.
Mechanism of action: Complement-mediated antibody-dependent killing of N. meningitidis.
Indications: To prevent invasive disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site pain, myalgia, erythema, fatigue, headache, induration, nausea and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal group B vaccine (rDNA, component, adsorbed).
Efficacy: 72% reduction in invasive MenB disease in infants in 2nd programme and 60% reduction in the 3rd programme.
Dosage:
Dose - 0.5 ml. Infants 2-5 months: two or three injections followed by booster.
Infants 6 to 11 months at time of first dose:  two injections with gap of at least 2 months and a booster.
Children 12 to 23 months :  two injections and a booster after an interval of 12 to 23 months.
After 2 years of age: two injections with interval of at least 1 month.
Mechanism of action: Specifically stimulating the body’s natural defence system.
Indications: To help protect against disease caused by the Neisseria meningitidis group B bacteria. Also recommended for people with some long-term health conditions who are at greater risk of complications from this disease.
Export: Distributed by - GlaxoSmithKline UK Limited, United Kingdom
Side effects of vaccine:
Very common (in children): pain, swelling, redness and hardness of the skin at the injection site, fever, loss of appetite, sleepiness, feeling irritable, unusual crying, vomiting, headache, diarrhoea, skin rash, painful joints.
Very common (in adults) : pain at the injection site, painful muscles and joints, nausea, headache and feeling unwell.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal group B vaccine.
Efficacy: Was assessed by measuring serum bactericidal activity using human complement (hSBA).
Vaccine formulation: Suspension in 0.5 mL single-dose prefilled syringe.
Dosage: Three-dose schedule: 0.5 mL at 0, 1-2, and 6 months.\nTwo-dose schedule: 0.5 mL at 0 and 6 months. If the second dose is given earlier than 6 months , a third dose should be given at least 4 months after the \nsecond dose.
Mechanism of action: Complement-mediated antibody dependent killing of N. meningitidis.
Indications: Prevents invasive disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B.
Side effects of vaccine: Pain at the injection site, fatigue, headache and muscle pain.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal (groups A, C, Y, W) conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: Was evaluated using a serogroup-specific serum bactericidal assay with exogenous human complement (hSBA).
Dosage: Dose - 0.5 ml. Primary Vaccination - a single dose\nBooster Vaccination - single dose in  individuals 15 years and older if at least 4 years have elapsed.
Mechanism of action: Production of bactericidal antibodies specific to the capsular polysaccharides of N. meningitidis serogroups A, C, W, and Y.
Indications: Prevention of invasive meningococcal disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A, C, W, and Y.
Side effects of vaccine: Children (2-9 years) : pain, erythema and swelling at injection site, malaise, myalgia and headache.\nAdolescents and adults (10 years and older): injection site pain, myalgia, headache and malaise.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal polysaccharide vaccine, groups A, C, Y and W-135.
Efficacy: For serogroup A : 87%\nFor serogroup C :  12% in children 6 to 23 months and 55% in children 24 to 36 months.
Vaccine formulation: Lyophilized vaccine, diluent used for reconstitution.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.\nSubjects who remain at risk should be revaccinated using a single dose every 3 years.
Mechanism of action: Production of bactericidal antibodies specific to the capsular polysaccharides of serogroups A, C, Y and W-135.
Indications: Invasive meningococcal disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A, C, Y, and W-135.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site pain, irritability, diarrhoea, headache, fatigue, malaise, arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal groups C and Y and Haemophilus b tetanus toxoid conjugate vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Solution for injection, lyophilized vaccine reconstituted with saline diluent.
Dosage: Four doses (0.5 mL each) at 2, 4, 6, and 12 to 15 months.
Mechanism of action: Production of bactericidal antibodies specific to the capsular polysaccharides of serogroups C and Y.
Indications: Prevents invasive disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroups C and Y and Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site pain, redness, swelling, irritability, drowsiness, loss of appetite and fever.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus type b and Meningococcal group C conjugate vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Powder and solvent for solution for injection.
Dosage: Single injection (0.5 ml). If first vaccination against Hib and MenC : three doses with a gap of at least four weeks.\nBooster protection: in the second year of life.
Mechanism of action: Works by causing the body to produce its own protection (antibodies) against these bacteria.
Indications: Prevents Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) and Neisseria meningitidis group C (MenC) bacteria diseases.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder- SmithKline Beecham Ltd
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, redness or swelling at the injection site, fever, irritability, loss of appetite and sleepiness.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal groups A, C, W-135 and Y conjugate vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Powder and a solvent for solution for injection.
Dosage:
Primary Immunisation -
Infants from 6 weeks to less than 6 months : Two injections given 2 months apart.
Infants from 6 months of age, children, adolescents and adults : One injection.
Booster Vaccination -
Infants from 6 weeks to less than 12 months of age: One booster dose at 12 months of age, at least 2 months after the last dose.
Mechanism of action: Helps the body to produce its own protection (antibodies) against the bacteria.
Indications: Protects against infections caused by Neisseria meningitidis types A, C, W-135 and Y.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder- Pfizer Europe MA EEIG
Side effects of vaccine: Fever, tiredness, headache, feeling drowsy, loss of appetite, swelling, pain and redness at the injection site.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal group C conjugate vaccine and contains a protein (called CRM197).
Dosage: For children 12 months and older : Single dose(0.5 mL)\nFor infants 2 to 12 months: Two doses, at least 2 months apart.
Indications: Protects against infections caused by Neisseria meningitidis type C.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder: GSK Vaccines S.r.l., Italy
Side effects of vaccine:
All age groups: Redness, swelling, pain at the injection site.
Infants: being sick(vomiting).
Infants and toddlers: irritability, drowsiness, difficulty sleeping, loss of appetite and diarrhoea.
Secondary school children: headache
Older children and adults: muscle and joint pains, feeling generally unwell

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal serogroup C oligosaccharide conjugate vaccine and contains a protein (called CRM197).
Efficacy: Short-term efficacy in infants, children and adolescents.
Dosage: 0.5 mL injection.\nFor infants 2 to 12 months: Two doses, at least 2 months apart.
Indications: Protects against infections caused by Neisseria meningitidis type C.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder: Nuron Biotech B.V., Amsterdam
Side effects of vaccine:
In all age groups - swelling, tenderness or pain at the injection site.
In infants and toddlers - loss of appetite, irritability, sleepiness or disturbances of sleeping patterns, being sick, diarrhoea.
In adults – headaches.
In pre-school children – fever.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcus serogroup A, C, W-135 and Y vaccine.
Efficacy: Vaccine effectiveness against serogroup A and W-135 was 95.8%.
Vaccine formulation: Powder and diluent for solution for injection.
Dosage: Single dose (0.5 mL).\nChildren who were aged under 5 years should be revaccinated every 2-3 years (if high risk).
Mechanism of action: Induces bactericidal antibodies against meningococci of the serogroups A, C, W-135 and Y.
Indications: Recommended for people at risk and those living in closed communities and contacts of patients with disease.
Side effects of vaccine: Irritability, drowsiness, headache,  pain and redness at the injection site, fatigue.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal polysaccharide vaccine (Group A, C, Y & W135)
Dosage: Single dose (0.5 mL).
Mechanism of action: Elicits bactericidal antibody response. Immunity lasts for three years.
Indications: Hajj and Umrah pilgrims heading to Mecca, Saudi Arabia, travellers heading to disease prone areas, military recruits and children and adults exposed to infected patients.

Description of the vaccine: Monovalent Meningococcal group C conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: No protective efficacy studies conducted with NeisVac-C vaccine.
Dosage: Infants 8 weeks to 11 months: Two doses 8 weeks apart. Booster at 12 months of age.\n12 months and under 7 years: Single dose. Booster dose if still at risk 2-3 years later.\nGreater than 7 years: Single dose. Booster dose if at risk 5 years later.
Indications: Prevention of invasive disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroup C.
Side effects of vaccine: In infants and children: Mild fever, decreased appetite, irritability, malaise.\nIn adults : Headache

Description of the vaccine: Anthrax vaccine adsorbed (AVA).
Efficacy: 92.5% for all types of anthrax disease.
Dosage:
Dose - 0.5 ml.
Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis -
Primary Series : 0,1 and 6 months
Booster Series : 6 and 12 months and at 12-month intervals.
Post-Exposure Prophylaxis -
Primary series : 0, 2 and 4 weeks.
Mechanism of action: Neutralizes the activities of the cytotoxic lethal toxin and edema toxin of bacteria.
Indications: Prevention of disease caused by Bacillus anthracis. Approved for pre-exposure prophylaxis and post-exposure prophylaxis.
Side effects of vaccine: Tenderness, pain, erythema, edema and arm motion limitation.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (Zoonotic disease through contact with an infected animal or by inhaling spores)

Description of the vaccine: Pneumococcal 20-valent conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: Assessed by measuring serotype-specific serum OPA of antibodies at 1-month post vaccination.
Dosage: Single dose (0.5 ml).
Indications: Prevention of pneumonia and invasive disease caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae serotypes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6A, 6B, 7F, 8, 9V, 10A, 11A, 12F, 14, 15B, 18C, 19A, 19F, 22F, 23F and 33F.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site pain and swelling, muscle pain, fatigue , headache and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Pneumococcal 13-valent conjugate vaccine (PCV13 ) [Diphtheria CRM197 Protein].
Dosage: Children (6 weeks to 5 years) : The four-dose series of 0.5 mL administered at 2, 4, 6, and 12-15 months.\nChildren (6 to 17 years ) and adults: a single dose.
Indications: Prevention of invasive disease caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae serotypes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6A, 6B, 7F, 9V, 14, 18C, 19A, 19F and 23F.
Side effects of vaccine: Infants, toddlers and children : irritability, injection site reactions, decreased appetite and sleep, increased sleep, fever.\nAdults (18 years and older) : injection site reactions, fatigue, headache, muscle and joint pain, decreased appetite, limitation of arm movement, vomiting, fever, chills and rash.\n

Description of the vaccine: Pneumococcal 7-valent conjugate vaccine (PCV 7) [Diphtheria CRM197 Protein].
Efficacy: 88.9% efficacy in intent-to-treat analysis for all pneumococcal serotypes (between 1995-1998).
Dosage:
0.5 ml dose.
For infants : three doses and fourth dose at 12-15 months, dosing interval is 4 to 8 weeks.
Previously unvaccinated children 7 months or older :
7-11 months of age  - 3 doses
12-23 months of age - 2 doses
Greater than or equal to 24 months to 9 years - 1 dose
Mechanism of action: Induces functional antibodies to all vaccine serotypes.
Indications: Prevents invasive disease caused by S. pneumoniae due to capsular serotypes included in the vaccine (4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F and 23F). Also indicated for protection  against otitis media.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site dermatitis, urticaria and pruritus,  lymphadenopathy localized to injection site, hypersensitivity reaction, crying, angioneurotic edema, erythema multiforme.

Description of the vaccine: Pneumococcal vaccine polyvalent (PPSV23).
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5-mL.
Mechanism of action: Enhances opsonization, phagocytosis and killing of pneumococci by leukocytes and other phagocytic cells.
Indications: Prevention caused by the 23 serotypes contained in the vaccine (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6B, 7F, 8, 9N, 9V, 10A, 11A, 12F, 14, 15B, 17F, 18C, 19F, 19A, 20, 22F, 23F, and 33F).
Side effects of vaccine: Injection-site reactions, headache, asthenia, fatigue and myalgia.

Description of the vaccine: Pneumococcal 15-valent conjugate vaccine.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.
Indications: Disease caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae serotypes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6A, 6B, 7F, 9V, 14, 18C, 19A, 19F, 22F, 23F and 33F.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection-site pain, swelling and erythema, fatigue, myalgia, headache and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: 10-valent Pneumococcal polysaccharide conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: Synflorix vaccine effectiveness was 97% against vaccine-type and 6A IPD and 71% against 19A IPD.
Dosage: Infants from 6 weeks to 6 months: Three dose primary series and a booster dose,  Infants from 7 to 11 months : Two doses and third dose in second year, Children from 12 to 23 months: Two doses.
Mechanism of action: The carrier proteins (PD, TT and DT) provide T-cell help to B-cells to produce a boostable antibody response of high affinity to the polysaccharide antigens.
Indications: Protects against invasive disease and acute otitis media caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Repurposing: For pneumococcal infections, acute otitis media.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness at the injection site, irritability, drowsiness, appetite lost, fever.

Description of the vaccine: Pneumococcal polysaccharide conjugate Vaccine (10-valent) for serotypes 1, 5, 6A, 6B, 7F, 9V, 14, 19A, 19F and 23F.
Name of the manufacturer: By collaboration between Serum Institute of India Pvt Ltd and PATH.
Vaccine formulation: Sterile suspension as 0.5 and 2.5 mL vial.
Dosage: Single 0.5 mL dose.
Indications: Immunization against invasive disease, pneumonia and acute otitis media.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus toxoids, acellular pertussis, inactivated poliovirus, haemophilus b conjugate and hepatitis B vaccine (DTaP vaccine).
Dosage: Three doses administered at 2, 4 and 6 months.
Mechanism of action: Post-vaccination anti-PRP level of greater than or equal to 0.15 mcg/mL : Minimal protective level\nAnti-PRP level of greater than or equal to 1.0 mcg/mL : Protection for 1-year period.
Indications: Prevents diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, poliomyelitis, hepatitis B, and invasive disease due to Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Export: Distributed by - Merck Sharp & Dohme Corp., USA and Sanofi Pasteur Inc., USA
Repurposing: Also for tetanus, pertussis, polio, diphtheria and hepatitis B.
Side effects of vaccine: Irritability, crying , injection site pain, erythema and swelling, somnolence, decreased appetite, fever, and vomiting.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Tetanus Toxoid Conjugate) (PRP-T).
Efficacy: Conducted with unconjugated Haemophilus b polysaccharide vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Lyophilized vaccine to be reconstituted with saline diluent.
Dosage: Four dose series (0.5 ml each). Primary series: One dose at 2, 4, and 6 months of age.\nBooster: One dose at 15 through 18 months.
Mechanism of action: Anti-PRP concentration of 0.15 mcg/mL : minimal protection\nAnti-PRP concentration of greater than or equal to 1.0 mcg/mL : Protection for 1 year
Indications: Prevention of invasive disease caused by Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Side effects of vaccine: Pain and redness, irritability, drowsiness, fever, loss of appetite, fussiness, and restlessness.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b conjugate vaccine (Tetanus toxoid conjugate) (PRP-T).
Vaccine formulation: Lyophilized powder to be reconstituted in supplied 0.4% Sodium Chloride diluent.
Dosage: Four dose series (0.5 ml each). Primary series: One dose at 2, 4, and 6 months.\nBooster: One dose at 15 through 18 months.\n
Mechanism of action: T-dependent immune response to antigens.\nanti-PRP of greater than 1.0 mcg/mL : Long-term protection
Indications: Prevention of invasive disease caused by Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Export: Distributed by - Sanofi Pasteur Inc., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Children (2 to 16 months) : irritability, inconsolable crying and lethargy.\nChildren (15-20 months): tenderness

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b conjugate vaccine (Tetanus toxoid conjugate) (PRP-T).
Name of the manufacturer: Pasteur Mérieux Serums & Vaccins S. A.
Dosage: Four dose series (0.5 ml each). Primary series: One dose at 2, 4, and 6 months.\nBooster: One dose at 15 through 18 months.
Indications: Prevention of invasive diseases caused by diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis and H influenzae type b.
Repurposing: Also for diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis.

Description of the vaccine: Meningococcal groups C and Y and Haemophilus b tetanus toxoid conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: Conducted with unconjugated Haemophilus b polysaccharide vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Single-dose vial of lyophilized vaccine reconstituted with saline diluent.
Dosage: Four doses (0.5 mL each) at 2, 4, 6, and 12 through 15 months.
Mechanism of action: Anti-PRP concentration of 0.15 mcg/mL : minimal protection\nAnti-PRP concentration of greater than or equal to 1.0 mcg/mL : Protection for 1 year
Indications: Prevents invasive disease caused by Neisseria meningitidis serogroups C and Y and Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site pain, redness, and swelling, irritability, drowsiness, loss of appetite and fever.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PRP-OMPC).
Efficacy: 93% for primary two doses. Efficacy for follow up period - 96.6% in children under 18 months and 100% in children over 18 months of age.
Dosage: Infants (2 to 14 months) : 0.5 mL dose at 2 months and a dose 2 months later. A booster dose (0.5 mL) at 12 to 15 months.\nChildren (15 months and older) : single 0.5 mL dose.
Indications: Routine vaccination against invasive disease caused by Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Export: Distributed by - Merck and Co. , INC.
Side effects of vaccine: Irritability, sleepiness, injection site pain, erythema, swelling, unusual high pitched crying, prolonged crying, diarrhoea, vomiting, otitis media, rash, and upper respiratory infection.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Inactivated Poliovirus and Haemophilus b Conjugate (Tetanus Toxoid Conjugate) vaccine \n(DTaP vaccine).
Name of the manufacturer: Sanofi Pasteur Limited and Sanofi Pasteur SA
Efficacy: Study with H. influenzae type b polysaccharide vaccine in Finland.
Dosage: Four dose immunization series administered at 2, 4, 6 and 15-18 months.
Mechanism of action: Anti-PRP level of 0.15 mcg/mL : Minimal protection\nGreater than 1.0 mcg/mL : protection through one-year period
Indications: The safety and effectiveness of Pentacel in infants less than 6 weeks of age and in children 5 to 16 years of age have not been established.
Export: Distributed by- Sanofi Pasteur Inc. , USA
Repurposing: For tetanus, pertussis, poliovirus and diphtheria.
Side effects of vaccine: Fussiness, inconsolable crying, fever, tenderness and increase in arm circumference.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus type b and Meningococcal group C conjugate vaccine.
Vaccine formulation: Powder and solvent for solution for injection
Dosage: Single injection (0.5 ml). If first vaccination against Hib and MenC : three doses with a gap of at least four weeks.\nBooster protection:  in the second year of life.
Mechanism of action: Works by causing the body to produce its own protection (antibodies) against these bacteria.
Indications: Prevents infectious diseases caused by Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) and Neisseria meningitidis group C (MenC) bacteria.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder- SmithKline Beecham Ltd
Side effects of vaccine: Very common - Pain, redness or swelling at the injection site, fever, irritability, loss of appetite and sleepiness.\nCommon - Injection site reaction, such as hard lump.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis (acellular, component), hepatitis B, poliomyelitis (inactivated) and Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) conjugate vaccine (adsorbed).
Dosage: Two or three injections with at least 2 or 1 month gap.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder- GlaxoSmithKline UK Limited
Repurposing: For Diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, hepatitis B, poliomyelitis.
Side effects of vaccine: Sleepiness, loss of appetite, high temperature, swelling, pain, redness, unusual crying, feeling irritable, diarrhoea, vomiting, feeling nervous.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b conjugate (meningococcal protein conjugate) and hepatitis b (recombinant) vaccine.
Efficacy: Efficacy similar to monovalent lyophilized PedvaxHIB.
Dosage: Three-dose regimen (2,4 and 12-15 months).
Indications: Haemophilus influenzae type b and all known subtypes of hepatitis B virus in infants born of HBsAg negative mothers.
Export: Distributed by - Merck Sharp & Dohme Corp., USA
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site reactions, fever, anorexia, diarrhoea, vomiting, irritability, somnolence, crying, upper respiratory infection, rhinorrhoea, cough, rhinitis, rash, otitis media.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b conjugate vaccine (tetanus toxoid conjugate) (HbOC).
Efficacy: Titer of antibody 0.15 µg/mL correlates with protection and greater than 1.0 µg/mL is associated with long-term protection.
Dosage: First dose at 2 to 6 months, followed by 2 more doses at least 8 weeks apart. Booster dose at 15 to 18 months.
Mechanism of action: IgG anti-HbPs antibodies of IgG1 subclass predominated.
Indications: Immunization of children against invasive diseases caused by H. influenzae type b.
Side effects of vaccine: More common : Diarrhoea, loss of appetite, vomiting.\nLess common : Earache, redness or swelling in the ear.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Diphtheria Toxoid-Conjugate) (PRP-D).
Efficacy: 90% protective efficacy in children 18 to 71 months.
Dosage: Single injection of 0.5 mL. Primary immunization at 15 months of age or as Booster vaccination at 12 to 15 months.
Mechanism of action: Induces Anti-capsular antibodies. Antibody concentration of 0.15 µg/mL  or more : Protection, 1 µg/mL or more : Long-term protection
Indications: Immunization against invasive diseases caused by Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Side effects of vaccine: Diarrhoea, vomiting, crying, fever, irritability, sleepiness, anorexia, urticaria, seizure, and renal failure.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis (acellular component), poliomyelitis (inactivated) and Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) conjugate vaccine (adsorbed).
Dosage: Two or three injections, gap of 1 month.
Indications: Immunisation against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, poliomyelitis and Haemophilus influenzae type b disease.
Export: Marketing authorisation holder - SmithKline Beecham Ltd
Repurposing: For Diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis and poliomyelitis.
Side effects of vaccine: Loss of appetite, high temperature, swelling, pain and redness, feeling restless, diarrhoea, hard lump, large swelling.

Description of the vaccine: DTPa/Hib vaccine.
Dosage: Single dose of Infanrix mixed with Hiberix.
Repurposing: For acellular pertussis, diphtheria and tetanus.

Description of the vaccine: Haemophilus influenzae type b conjugate vaccine.
Name of the manufacturer: Novartis Vaccines & Diagnostics S.r.l and GlaxoSmithKline
Dosage:
Primary Series-
Under 13 months : Three 0.5ml doses, gap of 4 weeks.
13 months and greater : Single 0.5 mL dose.
Booster- If 3 doses given before the age of 6 months, additional fourth dose should be given.
Mechanism of action: Antigenic capsular polysaccharides induce Ab production.
Indications: Not recommended for healthy children greater than 4 years.
Side effects of vaccine: Infants 2 to 6 months: tenderness, erythema, induration, unusual crying, irritability, vomiting, diarrhoea, change in eating habits, sleepiness, fever.\nChildren 12 to 15 months: unusual crying, irritability, vomiting, diarrhoea, change in eating habits, sleepiness, fever.\nBooster in children 16 to 20 months: irritability

Description of the vaccine: DTwP – HepB – Hib vaccine.
Efficacy: Predefined protective antibody level was 100% Hib short-term and 95% Hib long-term.
Dosage: Primary vaccination : 3 doses of 0.5 mL and one booster dose of 0.5 mL at 15-18 months.
Indications: Primary and booster immunisation against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, hepatitis B, and invasive illness caused by H. influenzae type b.
Export: Distributed by: GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals Limited, India
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis and hepatitis B recombinant.
Side effects of vaccine: Temporary swelling, tenderness and redness with fever, feeding disorders, sleepiness, irritability.

Description of the vaccine: DTaP-HepB-IPV-Hib fully liquid hexavalent vaccine.
Efficacy: Vaccine effectiveness was of 96.7% for the full primary series, and 98.5% for booster dose.
Dosage: Primary vaccination: Three doses of 0.5 ml and booster dose during the second year.
Mechanism of action: Make antibodies and the active substances are fixed onto aluminium compounds.
Indications: Primary and booster vaccination against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, hepatitis B, poliomyelitis and Haemophilus influenzae type b.
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, hepatitis B, poliomyelitis.
Side effects of vaccine: Pain and redness at the injection site, irritability and crying.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, Tetanus, Pertussis (Whole Cell), Hepatitis B (rDNA) and Haemophilus influenzae type b conjugate vaccine.
Dosage: Three doses of 0.5 ml, at least 4 weeks gap.
Mechanism of action: Boosts the immune system and produces protective antibodies.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, hepatitis B and haemophilus influenzae type b.
Export: Marketed by : Sanofi Healthcare India Private Limited
Approval: Central Drugs Standard Control Organization, India and WHO pre-qualified.
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis and hepatitis B.
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, redness and swelling at injection site, fever, vomiting, crying, drowsiness, loss of appetite and irritability.
Post vaccination: Vaccinee to be medically supervised for at least 30 minutes after vaccination.

Description of the vaccine: Act-HIB reconstituted with TRIPACEL is Diphtheria, tetanus, acellular pertussis, Haemophilus influenzae type b conjugate vaccine.
Dosage: Three dose immunization series, interval of 2 months, followed by a fourth dose.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis and invasive Haemophilus influenzae type b infections.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder: Sanofi Pasteur Ltd, Bangkok, Thailand
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus and acellular pertussis.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site tenderness, swelling, redness, fever, fussiness, irritability, less active, eating less, crying, diarrhoea.

Description of the vaccine: Pentavalent vaccine (DTwP-HepB-Hib).
Vaccine formulation: Sterile and uniform suspension in multidose vial.
Dosage: First dose as early as 6 weeks and two subsequent doses given at 4 week interval.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus, whole cell pertussis, hepatitis B and Haemophilus influenzae type b infections.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder: Panacea Biotech Ltd, India
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus, whole cell pertussis, hepatitis B.
Side effects of vaccine: Temporary swelling, tenderness and redness together with fever, feeding disorders, sleepiness, irritability

Description of the vaccine: Adsorbed diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis (acellular component), inactivated poliomyelitis and Haemophilus influenzae type b conjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: 92.2–100% of infants achieved seroprotective levels of anti-PRP (greater than or equal to 0.15 µg/ml) antibodies.
Dosage: Two injections at 2 and 4 months of age and a booster at the age of 11 months OR three injections, gap of 1 to 2 months and booster within the second year of life.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus, acellular pertussis, inactivated poliomyelitis and Hib.
Export: Marketing Authorisation Holder: Sanofi Pasteur SA, France
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus, acellular pertussis, inactivated poliomyelitis and Hib.
Side effects of vaccine: Injection site redness, swelling, pain, fever, vomiting, irritability, abnormal crying, nervousness, loss of appetite, somnolence.

Description of the vaccine: Diphtheria, tetanus toxoids, acellular pertussis vaccine adsorbed combined with inactivated poliomyelitis vaccine and haemophilus b conjugate vaccine \n(Tetanus protein conjugate).
Efficacy: The anti-PRP seroprotection rate (antibody concentration over 0.15 mg/mL) was 67.7% after a two or three dose primary series.
Vaccine formulation: White to off-white suspension for injection
Dosage: Primary vaccination: Two or three dose series, gap of at least 1 month.\nBooster vaccination: Single dose, 6 months after the last dose.
Mechanism of action: T-dependent immune response with induction of immunological priming and memory.
Indications: Immunization against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, poliomyelitis and Haemophilus influenzae type b disease.
Repurposing: For diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, poliomyelitis and Hib.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, swelling or tenderness, irritability, less activity, vomiting, unusual high-pitched crying, fever, appetite loss, diarrhoea.

Age: 15 years and older (who work in high-risk occupations)
Description of the vaccine: Formalin-inactivated phase I Coxiella burnetii vaccine.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.
Mechanism of action: Early antibody response is predominantly with the IgM subclass, IgG antibodies appear later and cell mediated immunity develops.
Indications: Recommended for people working with cattle, sheep or goats, or products from these animals.
Side effects of vaccine: Redness, itchiness, pain, burning, swelling, headaches, fever, muscle ache, fatigue, nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea and increased sweating.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (by inhaling dust particles contaminated by infected animals)

Description of the vaccine: Recombinant four-antigen Mycobacterium leprae antigen LEP-F1 in combination with the adjuvant formulation GLA-SE.
Dosage: Three doses on days 0, 28, and 56.
Mechanism of action: LEP-F1-specific IgG antibodies and generated LEP-F1-specific Th1 cells.
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, erythema, induration,  headache, arthralgia, chills, loss of appetite, fever, fatigue and myalgia.

Description of the vaccine: Recombinant Plague vaccine.
Name of the manufacturer: DynPort Vaccine Company LLC, A GDIT Company
Dosage: Single dosage and two 3-dose schedules.
Side effects of vaccine: Transient edema and erythema at the injection site.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (enzootic from wild rodents and their fleas)

Description of the vaccine: Plague vaccine, Flagellin/F1/V, phosphate-buffered saline, pH 6.2.
Name of the manufacturer: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)
Dosage: Administered on days 0 and 28 at a dose of 1, 3, 6, or 10 micrograms (mcg).
Mechanism of action: Macrophage-protective antibody and significant CD4+ T-cell responses.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (enzootic from wild rodents and their fleas)

Description of the vaccine: Recombinant Botulinum vaccine A/B.
Name of the manufacturer: DynPort Vaccine Company LLC, A GDIT Company
Dosage: 0.5 mL dose at Days 0, 28 and 182.
Mechanism of action: BoNT neutralizing antibodies bind to BoNT and prevent its action at the cholinergic neurons.
Indications: Protects from BoNT serotype A, subtype A1 (BoNT/A1) and BoNT serotype B, subtype B1 (BoNT/B1).
Side effects of vaccine: Transient mild inflammation at the injection site.

Description of the vaccine: Multivalent recombinant OspA (Outer Surface Protein A) based vaccine.
Dosage: Three (month 0-2-6) or two (month 0-6) vaccinations.
Mechanism of action: Targets the outer surface protein A (OspA) of the bacteria.
Indications: Protects from six serotypes of Borrelia.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (from the bite of infected blacklegged ticks)

Description of the vaccine: Multivalent recombinant OspA Lyme Borreliosis vaccine.
Dosage: Three primary doses and one booster dose.
Interspecies transfer: Yes (from the bite of infected blacklegged ticks)

Description of the vaccine: 4-component Neisseria meningitidis group B vaccine (4CMenB).
Name of the manufacturer: University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill
Dosage: Two-doses, second dose at 5th week.
Mechanism of action: Induces antibodies in humans that recognize gonococcal proteins.

Description of the vaccine: Recombinant protein subunit adjuvanted vaccine.
Bacterial strain: Gram-negative bacteria, coccoid or rod shape.
Dosage: Four vaccinations.

Description of the vaccine: Two S. aureus toxoids - protein rAT and protein rLukS-PV.
Name of the manufacturer: Uniformed Services University of the Health Sciences
Dosage: Single dose of monovalent or bivalent toxoid vaccine.

Description of the vaccine: S. aureus 4-antigen vaccine (SA4Ag).
Name of the manufacturing country: Austria, Bulgaria, Canada, France, Germany, Hungary, Japan, Spain, Sweden, United Kingdom, United States
Dosage: Single dose.

Description of the vaccine: 3-Antigen S. aureus vaccine.
Dosage: Three doses and a booster.
Mechanism of action: Generates antibodies that inhibit the Fg binding of invasive S. aureus.

Description of the vaccine: Recombinant protein vaccine.
Dosage: Two doses, 6 months apart at two dose levels.
Mechanism of action: Increases in CD3+ T-cell and neutrophil infiltration and IL-17A, IL-22 and host defence peptide expression.

Description of the vaccine: Bivalent S. aureus glycoconjugate vaccine.
Efficacy: 64% through 32 weeks of follow-up and 57% through 40 weeks of follow-up.
Dosage: Two doses.
Indications: Treatment of infections in kidney patients receiving peritoneal dialysis and are prone to serious staphylococcal infections.

Description of the vaccine: Containing S. aureus 0657nI iron surface determinate B (IsdB).
Name of the manufacturing country: Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Colombia, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Guatemala, Ireland, Italy, Japan, Korea, Republic of, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Norway, Peru, Russian Federation, Singapore, Spain, Sweden, Taiwan, United Kingdom, United States
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.
Indications: Prevents serious Staphylococcus aureus infections following elective cardiothoracic surgery.

Description of the vaccine: Pentavalent S. aureus vaccine.
Dosage: NA
Mechanism of action: Induces antibodies that target S. aureus capsular polysaccharides types 5, 8 and the cell wall antigen type 336.
Indications: Prevention and treatment of S. aureus infections.

Description of the vaccine: Typhoid Vi Polysaccharide vaccine.
Dosage: Single dose of 0.5 mL.
Repurposing: For typhoid fever and bacterial infections.
Side effects of vaccine: Pain, erythema, swelling, fever, headache, malaise and myalgia.

Description of the vaccine: Chlamydia vaccine.
Dosage: Three doses.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella flexneri 2a live vaccine.
Dosage: Three doses, one month apart.
Mechanism of action: Anti-lipopolysaccharide responses, interferon gamma production in response to Shigella antigens.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella sonnei vaccine.
Dosage: Three doses, gap of 4 weeks and one booster dose.
Indications: Prevents the disease caused by Shigella sonnei.
Other Name: 1790GAHB vaccine, GSK-3536852A, GSK-3902986A, GVGH Shigella sonnei 1790GAHB vaccine.

Description of the vaccine: Formalin-inactivated whole cell Shigella flexneri 2a killed vaccine.
Dosage: Three doses.
Mechanism of action: IgA and IgG responses to LPS, Th1 and Th17 cytokines.
Side effects of vaccine: Loose stools, diarrhoea, abdominal pain, bloating, defecation urgency, nausea, vomiting, headache, malaise, chills, myalgia, reactive arthritis and constipation.

Description of the vaccine: Monovalent Synthetic Carbohydrate-based.
Dosage: Two doses, gap of 4 weeks.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella sonnei-rEPA and flexneri2a rEPA vaccines.
Name of the manufacturer: Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD)
Efficacy: S. sonnei-rEPA induced an overall efficacy of 74%.
Dosage: Two doses, gap of 6 weeks.
Other Name: S. sonnei O-SP-rEPA conjugate, S. flexneri 2a O-SP-rEPA conjugate.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella Flexneri 2a vaccine and can function as an adjuvant.
Name of the manufacturer: U.S. Army Medical Research and Development Command
Dosage: Three doses.
Mechanism of action: Mucosal immune response, systemic IgG response and generate antigen-specific memory B cells.
Indications: Vaccine against diarrhoea, dysentery and fever.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella sonnei O-SP-core vaccine.
Name of the manufacturer: Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD)
Efficacy: 71.1% efficacy in 3-4 year-old. No efficacy for recipients of S. flexneri 2a.
Dosage: Single dose.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella Flexneri 2a vaccine.
Dosage: Two doses (0.5 mL), gap of 4 weeks.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella Sonnei vaccine.
Name of the manufacturer: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)
Dosage: One or two doses.
Mechanism of action: Serum anti-LPS, anti-Invaplex IgG and IgA.
Side effects of vaccine: Diarrhoea, fever, headache, cramps, vomiting, myalgia and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella Sonnei vaccines.
Name of the manufacturer: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)
Dosage: Single dose.
Mechanism of action: Serum anti-LPS, anti-Invaplex IgG and IgA.
Side effects of vaccine: Diarrhoea, fever, headache, cramps, vomiting, myalgia and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Combined Shigella-ETEC vaccine.
Dosage: Single dose
Mechanism of action: Anti-Shigella LPS IgG, anti-CFA/I antibodies production.
Indications: Prevents disease outcomes-weight loss and diarrhoea.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella dysenteriae vaccine.
Dosage: Two doses, gap of 8 weeks.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella dysenteriae type-1 vaccine.
Dosage: Two doses.
Mechanism of action: IgA and IgG LPS-specific ASCs and antibody response.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella flexneri 2a strain SC602.
Dosage: Single dose.
Mechanism of action: IgA ASC response and a threefold or greater rise in serum IgA antibody, urinary sIgA responses in addition to IgG ASC and IgG serum responses.
Indications: Protection against fever and severe shigellosis.
Approval: Conducted under Food and Drug Administration investigational new drug application.
Side effects of vaccine: Abdominal cramps, nausea, emesis, tenesmus, gas, headache, myalgia, arthralgia, loss of appetite and fatigue.

Description of the vaccine: Constructed from wild-type S.flexneri 2a strain.
Dosage: Single dose.

Description of the vaccine: Shigella-tetravalent vaccine.
Dosage: Two doses (for adults). Three doses (for children).

Description of the vaccine: Shigella Sonnei vaccine.
Name of the manufacturer: U.S. Army Medical Research and Development Command
Dosage: Single dose.
Side effects of vaccine: Loose stool, diarrhoea, dysentery, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain and cramps, bloating, constipation, fever, chills, headache, light-headedness, generalized myalgia, malaise, decreased appetite, excess flatulence, reactive arthritis and arthralgia.

Description of the vaccine: Artificial Invaplex vaccine consists of recombinant IpaB, IpaC and wild-type LPS using molar ratios.
Name of the manufacturer: U.S. Army Medical Research and Development Command
Dosage: Three doses.
Mechanism of action: Systemic and mucosal vaccine antigen-specific immune response.

Description of the vaccine: S.Flexneriza-S.Sonnei bivalent conjugate vaccine.
Name of the manufacturer: Beijing Zhifei Lvzhu Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd
Dosage: Single dose.

Description of the vaccine: This vaccine is an aro-D mutant derivative of EcSf2a-1.
Dosage: Three doses.
Mechanism of action: IgA or IgG antibody titers recognizing purified LPS.

Description of the vaccine: Killed whole-cell Leptospiral vaccine.
Dosage: Four to eight doses.\nUsual dose - Two injections 15 days apart, followed by a booster 4 to 6 months later.
